extra.reason: Added libmpv-dev to release.yml Linux apt-get block; Linux release leg can now link libmpv.

ci.yml now installs Linux system deps (webkit2gtk/rsvg/ssl/libmpv-dev) and runs 'cargo build' for src-tauri on ubuntu, catching Linux-only compile breaks. Verified locally: cargo build exit 0, binary links libmpv.so.2 + libwebkit2gtk-4.1.

Created src-tauri/tauri.linux.conf.json with deb/rpm libmpv runtime depends (libmpv2|libmpv1, mpv-libs) + appimage.bundleMediaFramework. transparent:false moved to duna.3 (needs Wayland testing + array-merge caveat).
release.yml Linux leg installs libmpv-dev (line 58); cargo links system libmpv. Verified in committed workflow.
ci.yml compiles src-tauri on Linux with libmpv-dev + webkit2gtk deps (steps 'Install src-tauri system deps (Linux)' + 'src-tauri build (Linux)'). Verified in committed workflow.

tauri.linux.conf.json now sets app.windows[0].transparent=false (full window object required — RFC7396 merge replaces arrays wholesale; visible:false/title/geometry preserved). Bundle deb/rpm libmpv depends already present. Verified merge + Linux cargo build green.

Import-gated/split Windows-only imports; cfg_attr(not(windows),allow(dead_code)) on inert consts/fields/fns/methods (no deletions, struct layout + Windows behavior unchanged). ci.yml Linux step tightened to 'cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings'. Verified clean on Linux.
All gates green on Linux: tsc --noEmit clean; vitest 1718/1718; relay-server clippy -D warnings clean; src-tauri clippy -D warnings clean (was blocked on duna.5); src-tauri cargo build clean.
ci.yml compiles src-tauri on Linux (libmpv-dev + webkit2gtk deps) and now gates on clippy -D warnings. A Linux-only compile break fails CI. Verified in committed workflow.

Self-hosted libmpv-vendor release on nwilliams22/prexu mirrors the baseline dev archive; ci.yml + release.yml repointed at it with pinned SHA256. Windows headless-mpv job green (fetch from mirror succeeded). Durable — asset is ours, excluded from delete-older-releases.

Wayland HTML5 codec-gap captured in notes: only H.264/AAC direct-plays; HEVC(8/10-bit), AV1, and AC3/E-AC3/TrueHD/DTS audio all force Plex transcode (~17% of movies by video, ~170 titles by audio) = Phase-2 native-player scope. Also surfaced + filed prexu-z5mz (Wayland DMABUF launch crash) and confirmed the transparent:false opaque-window fix. Acceptance met (gap list in issue).

Fixed in #27 (squash-merged to main): set WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 at top of run() on Linux when unset. Verified live on KDE Plasma Wayland + NVIDIA — clean launch with no external env var, Error 71 gone, window renders.

Known native-Wayland limitation (client can't position its own toplevel; tauri-plugin-window-state position-restore is a no-op there, size still restores). No reliable in-app fix. KWin window-rule workaround documented in docs/linux-dev.md (Wayland runtime notes), with kscreen-doctor steps to find the monitor origin. Revisit if Tauri/wry expose a Wayland positioning protocol.

Fixed in #29 (merged): watchedToggleActions() makes the Continue Watching context menu offer both Mark as Watched + Mark as Unwatched for any in-progress item, regardless of prior watched state. Unit tested + verified live on Wayland (marking an already-watched in-progress movie clears it from Continue Watching).
Render-API integration complete: HEVC 10-bit direct-plays via nvdec under the webview on Wayland, no leaks/crashes on resize/teardown, brightness source-accurate. X11 checkbox split to prexu-5jxx.
Acceptance met on Wayland: render context + frame loop shipped in PR #31, all smoke defects root-caused (DMABUF retained-composite) and hardware-verified; X11 verification split to prexu-5jxx
Engine selection/fallback/Settings toggle shipped in PR #31; controls auto-hide hardware-verified; attribution logging kept permanently with mousemove re-arm demoted to trace (PR #32)

Fixed and verified on hardware 2026-07-03: two root causes — (1) detach_mpv freed the mpv render context without gl_area.make_current(), corrupting GDK's GL/compositing state (window stopped committing buffers → frozen on last frame, black after resize); (2) card-enter 'both' fill kept finished transform animations permanently active on every poster card, livelocking webkit2gtk 2.52 in CSS transition retargeting on any document-wide class flip. User verified two consecutive play->stop cycles (button + Esc) with no freeze.

Fixed and verified on hardware 2026-07-03: circular render/vo deadlock in mpv's block-for-target-time wait, triggered by exit_minimize's video-margin-ratio writes mid-playback. Fix: vendored libmpv2 4.1.0 adding RenderContext::render_no_block (BLOCK_FOR_TARGET_TIME=0), used by the Linux compositor render handler; plus busy/ready parity on Linux exit_minimize. User verified: expand restores immediately, video stays live; M7 (stop while mini -> replay full-frame) and M8 (fullscreen interplay after minimize) both pass. Remaining visual staging tracked in prexu-hg1j.

Fixed in PR #36 (main 46de2d3): host-window-ready now emitted from the GLArea render handler on the first rendered frame per load (armed at first PlaybackRestart, disarmed on FileLoaded/detach). On-hardware verify steps recorded in issue comment / axj4.5 test plan.

Fixed in PR #37: sub-scale set to mini video height fraction + sub-use-margins=no in apply_margin_ratios (single choke point for enter/update/exit/resize-while-mini); zero ratios restore defaults. On-hardware verify: subs in mini mode should render proportionally small inside the mini rect.

Fixed in PR #38: mute persists to localStorage (prexu_muted) like volume, in both engines. Fresh sessions initialize from storage; toggleMute and setVolume auto-unmute persist. On-hardware verify (A3): mute → stop → play next item should stay muted after the reveal.

Resolved as hypothesis (b): mute is per-session BY DESIGN (bd memory player-mute-scope-decision) — kept across in-session episode handoff/autoplay, deliberately lost on stop. PR #38 (persistence) was wrong and reverted by PR #39, which documents the decision in code and pins the per-session reset with a regression test. In-mount handoff retention already worked and is covered by reveal-mute test (a). No behavior bug remains.

Fixed in PR #40: Linux skips the three WebView2-motivated staging mechanisms — busy-window class drop + 2xrAF ready deferral (useTransparentWindow), wait-for-IPC restore flip (PlayerContext), and the early opaque paint on exit (prepareNavAway). Windows/HTML5 unchanged. On-hardware verify steps in issue comment; if residual delay remains, instrument player_unload IPC duration next.

Implemented in PR #41 as a main-window morph via Tauri/GTK ops — the second-GTK-window premise was stale (Windows popout never used a separate window; the single-surface GLArea follows the window allocation). X11 full parity; Wayland floats without placement/keep-above (documented). On-hardware verify plan in issue comment.

Fixed in PR #42: LoadStats replaced with a zeroed LoaderStats literal; all hls.js imports type-only. Measured: Player chunk 675KB -> 153KB; hls.js is its own async chunk loaded only when the HTML5 engine mounts; Vite hoist warning gone. (Correction vs the audit text: in the current graph hls.js was hoisted into the lazy Player chunk, not the entry chunk — same defect, cost was per player open on the native path.)

Fixed in PR #43: stream_body fixed 256KiB-buffer relay for both /local files and proxied Plex responses (peak memory constant, first byte immediate); parse_range honors explicit ends + suffix ranges (old code read seek-offset->EOF into one Vec — OOM class); upstream Content-Length forwarded, EOF-delimited otherwise; zero-alloc Range header detection. 10 new unit tests.

Fixed in PR #43: stream_body fixed 256KiB-buffer relay for /local files and proxied Plex responses (peak memory constant, first byte immediate); parse_range honors explicit ends + suffix ranges; upstream Content-Length forwarded, EOF-delimited otherwise; zero-alloc Range header detection. 10 new unit tests.

Fixed in PR #44: warmup cfg broadened to any(windows, linux); background thread is safe (attach_mpv marshals to GTK main thread); warmed idle mpv renders nothing (pump gate quiescent) and reveal only arms at PlaybackRestart. On-hardware verify: first-play latency after fresh launch should drop to ~loadfile time.

Fixed in PR #44: event pump observes idle-active + mirrors pause/idle-active into PUMP_PAUSED/PUMP_IDLE atomics in linux_compositor; should_pump_now is two relaxed loads (was 2 FFI property reads per 16ms tick); mirrors reset on detach. Unit test added.
Fixed in PR #44: relay AppState.http shared client (8 TMDb handlers + WS auth via param); app downloads share a OnceLock client + 256KiB tokio BufWriter (flush already existed before rename).
Merged in PR #43 (3e51ede): streaming proxy for local-file + proxied responses
Merged in PR #44 (29790cf): event-driven pump gate with observed atomics
Merged in PR #44 (29790cf): shared reqwest::Client in relay AppState
PR #46: spinner scoped to /play/* departures via useRouteTransitionSpinner (150ms pre-show delay, 300ms ceiling elsewhere); player-exit last-frame-hold path untouched

PR #46: success toast on download complete, failure toast after retries exhaust, none on cancel; toast dispatcher threaded into useDownloadsState via AppProviders
